ANACIM Senegal; investigation arm is now BEA Senegal; no online accident report archive found, only climate/weather PDFs on anacim.sn.
ANAC Côte d'Ivoire (anac.ci); Wayback has regulatory PDFs but no accident investigation reports confirmed publicly accessible.
ANAC Mali (anacmali.org); website has no accident report section; CDX for anac.ml returned empty; BEA France led notable investigations (AH5017).
ANAC Burkina Faso (anac.bf); Wayback captures only ISO cert and flight schedules, no accident investigation reports.
ANAC Niger (anac.ne); Wayback captures only regulatory texts (RTAC-14), no accident investigation reports found.
ANAC Togo (anac-togo.tg); enquetes-accidents page lists only enabling decrees and procedures, zero published investigation reports.
BEA Bénin (bea.bj); live archive with 5 published investigation reports (2003–2025) including final reports with downloadable PDFs; Wayback confirms 5+ PDF captures.
ANAC Mauritanie (anac.mr); Wayback captures only AIP documents, no accident investigation reports found online.
ANAC Tchad (anac.td); single Wayback capture is a PDF viewer test file; no accident investigation reports found; very limited institutional capacity.
Central African Republic civil aviation (ACAC/ANAC-CF); AfDB support project noted but no online accident archive found; CDX returned no captures.
ANAC Gabon (anacgabon.org); site references AIG accident forms but no published investigation reports; Wayback captures only contacts/CEMAC code PDFs.
BEA Congo (bea.cg); live archive with 23 investigations (2007–2023), 19 final reports published; Wayback confirms 5+ PDF captures.
DR Congo (RVA/DGRAD); among world's highest accident rates; no functional online investigation archive; CDX for rva.aero returned empty.
Equatorial Guinea ACNAE; very limited institutional capacity; CDX for acnae.gq and anacgq.gq returned empty; no online archive found.
ANAC Angola/INAVIC (anac.ao); Wayback has regulatory PDFs (AIP, NT-12 accident notification); accidentincident.pdf is AIP ENR 1.14 procedures, not investigation reports.
Kenya AAID (aaid.transport.go.ke) publishes preliminary and final reports; Wayback has PDFs incl. 2025 captures (civil aviation act docs; accident reports confirmed per sub-agent CDX run).
Tanzania AAIB has no standalone domain; TCAA (tcaa.go.tz) hosted accident PDFs; Wayback confirmed bell206-03.pdf (2005) and Apr30DER.pdf (2006).
Uganda AAIIU section exists at works.go.ug but no confirmed accident PDFs in Wayback; live scrape of Ministry publications required.
Ethiopia EAIB publishes reports at ecaa.gov.et including ET302 preliminary/final; Wayback has 1,251+ PDF captures; admin/regulatory docs dominate but ET302 chain confirmed.
Rwanda AAID portal has real accident reports; Wayback confirmed 9XR-SI and 9XR-SJ Robinson R44 investigation PDFs (captured 2022-2023).
Burundi AACB (aacb.gov.bi) intermittently offline; no Wayback captures; no public accident report archive found.
Sudan AAICD under SCAA; website displaced to Port Sudan due to active conflict; Wayback CDX returned only Arabic announcement PDF, no accident reports.
Somalia SCAA has no report section; major accidents delegated to state-of-registry authority (varies); no Wayback captures on scaa.gov.so.
Zambia AAIB (aaib.org.zm) publishes final reports; Wayback confirmed 9J-KMN.pdf (captured Dec 2024, Dec 2025, Apr 2026), 3 versions archived.
Zimbabwe CAAZ (caaz.co.zw) is combined regulator/investigator; Wayback PDFs are exam results only, no accident reports found; no confirmed public report archive.
Malawi MCAA established 2017; VP Chilima 2024 crash investigated by presidential commission + Germany BFU; no captures on caa.gov.mw or transport.gov.mw.
Botswana DAI publishes reports via gov.bw; 2024/2025 PDFs confirmed live; CAAB Wayback has opaque download URLs (doc ID scheme) from 2011-2017.
Namibia DAAII (mwt.gov.na) publishes reports from 2003 to present; Wayback CDX confirmed PDF captures; accident-specific PDFs need filtered enumeration.
Morocco BEAM under DGAC publishes accident reports at aviationcivile.gov.ma; Wayback confirmed 2013-2014 Docs/ PDFs (regulatory/certification mix).

Algeria ANAC Direction Enquetes (anac.dz/en/investigation-reports/) publishes final reports; new directorate (2024); Wayback CDX confirmed PDF captures on anac.dz (Dec 2024 dates); historical accidents (AH5017) investigated by France BEA.

Tunisia BE-DGAC (be.dgac@mt.gov.tn) exists under Ministry of Transport but no public online report archive found; Tuninter 1153 (2005) report published by Italy ANSV; no Wayback captures on oaca.nat.tn or mt.gov.tn.
DGAC Chile operates SIASA accident database at dgac.cl — structured, searchable, publicly accessible; CDX confirms PDF captures present.
Aerocivil (UAEAC) publishes investigation reports at aerocivil.gov.co; site confirmed live May 2026 via Wayback availability.
DGAC Ecuador (aviacioncivil.gob.ec) publishes some investigation PDFs; CDX shows 3 captures but content is mixed regulatory/accident; site live May 2026.
DGAC under Peru MTC (mtc.gob.pe) publishes aeronautical bulletins and quality docs; CDX confirmed 3 PDF captures; accident investigation section hard to navigate.
DINACIA (dinacia.gub.uy) site live May 2026; CDX shows download links archived; publishes some accident reports but no dedicated structured archive.
AAC (aac.gob.sv) publishes individual investigation PDFs via WordPress downloads; CDX confirmed accident report PDFs (aac-accid-002-2016, aac-003-2016); no structured index.
DGAC Costa Rica (dgac.go.cr) — last Wayback snapshot 2024; CDX PDF check timed out; no known public accident investigation archive confirmed.
DGAC Guatemala (dgac.gob.gt) — old domain dgacguatemala.com has no Wayback presence; CDX returned 0 captures on both domains; no public accident archive confirmed.
IDAC (idac.gob.do) — no Wayback availability snapshot returned; CDX PDF check timed out; no publicly accessible accident investigation archive found.
JCAA (jcaa.gov.jm) site live May 2026; CDX shows annual reports and admin PDFs but no dedicated accident investigation archive; US-registered aircraft go to NTSB.
INAC Nicaragua (inac.gob.ni) — site confirmed live Apr 2026 via Wayback; CDX shows only 2010-2011 doc files, no accident investigation PDFs; no public archive.
TTCAA (ttcaa.co.tt) — no Wayback availability snapshot; CDX PDF check returned empty array; no confirmed online accident investigation archive.
CAA Suriname (caas.sr) — no Wayback availability snapshot returned; CDX PDF check timed out; very small authority, no confirmed online archive.
INAC Venezuela (inac.gob.ve) — Wayback returned empty snapshots object; CDX PDF check returned no output; domain effectively offline due to Venezuela's infrastructure collapse.
IACC Cuba (iacc.cu) — Wayback returned empty snapshots; CDX PDF check returned [] (zero captures); no public internet presence or accessible archive.
AAIB India (aaib.gov.in) publishes final accident/incident investigation reports; CDX confirmed 5+ PDFs including regulatory documents and reports; active since ~2010.
Pakistan AIB (aib.gov.pk) exists but CDX returned empty; CAAP (caapakistan.com.pk) has archived PDFs (aeronautical docs from 2010-2011); accident reports sporadic, site stability low.
CAAB Bangladesh (caab.gov.bd) has archived PDFs from 2013-2015 in CDX (5 confirmed); no dedicated accident investigation body found; reports irregular and may not be final investigation reports.
CAASL Sri Lanka (caasl.lk) has archived PDFs in CDX (4 confirmed from 2004) but these are regulatory/stats docs; no dedicated AIB; accident reports not publicly archived, contact-based only.

CAAN Nepal (caanepal.gov.np) CDX returned no PDFs; AIBN exists but no public archive found; Nepal historically high accident rate (mountainous terrain) but reports not systematically published online.
DCAB Bhutan (dcab.gov.bt) CDX returned no PDFs; very limited aviation activity, no dedicated accident investigation unit found; investigations likely delegated ad-hoc.
CAM Maldives (cam.gov.mv) CDX returned 5 PDFs but only telecom regulatory standards; no aviation accident investigation authority or public accident reports found; ICAO category low-activity state.
KNKT Indonesia (knkt.go.id) is a prolific publisher of aviation accident and serious incident reports in English; CDX confirmed 5 archived PDFs; one of the best accident archives in Southeast Asia.

MAAIB Malaysia publishes investigation reports; CDX for maaib.gov.my returned empty but alternate domains timed out (CDX unverified); MAAIB known to publish English-language reports publicly per ICAO annex 13.
AAIC Thailand (aaic.or.th) publishes accident investigation reports; CDX timed out on both attempts (CDX unverified); known to publish English summaries and Thai-language full reports.
CAAV Vietnam (caav.gov.vn) CDX returned no PDFs; no dedicated AIB website found; investigation reports occasionally published in Vietnamese via CAAV but not systematically archived publicly.
CAAP Philippines (caap.gov.ph) CDX confirmed 5 archived PDF downloads from 2019; AAIIB publishes reports via CAAP website; coverage patchy but publicly available.

Myanmar DCA/MAIB (dca.gov.mm) CDX confirmed 6+ accident investigation final reports from 2013-2017 including AIG reports on runway excursions; site likely degraded post-2021 coup but archive preserved.
SSCA Cambodia (ssca.gov.kh) CDX returned no PDFs; no public accident investigation archive found; Cambodia typically defers to ICAO facilitation for major investigations.
DCA Laos (dca.gov.la) CDX returned no PDFs; no public aviation accident investigation archive found; limited aviation activity and institutional capacity for public reporting.
CABD Brunei (cabd.gov.bn) CDX returned no PDFs; very limited aviation activity; no dedicated public accident investigation archive found.
Timor-Leste has no domestic accident investigation capability; ATSB Australia acts as delegate under bilateral agreement; AAOTL.tl CDX returned no PDFs; reports via atsb.gov.au if any.

Japan Transport Safety Board (JTSB) publishes full investigation reports at jtsb.mlit.go.jp/aircraft/. English summaries available for most reports. CDX verified: PDF aircraft reports archived (e.g. /aircraft/download/58-3-JA8061-01.pdf). Active archive dating back decades.

Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board (ARAIB) at araib.go.kr publishes Korean-language reports with English summaries. CDX check returned empty (no archived PDFs found in Wayback CDX), wayback_target omitted. Reports are available directly on the live site; Korean-language parsing required for full detail.

Taiwan Transportation Safety Board (TTSB, formerly ASC) at asc.gov.tw publishes accident investigation reports. CDX verified: PDFs archived from asc.gov.tw. Reports available in Chinese with English summaries for major accidents. Site restructured over time; legacy asc.gov.tw remains the main archivable domain.

CAAC Aviation Accident Investigation Division (AAISSG) publishes selected reports at caac.gov.cn. CDX verified: PDFs archived from caac.gov.cn (regulatory/safety docs confirmed). Archive is selective — major accidents published, minor incidents often absent. Reports are in Chinese; English translations rare. Access can be inconsistent for older reports.

Mongolia Civil Aviation Authority (MCAA) at mcaa.gov.mn is the regulator; dedicated accident investigation unit is the AIB under MCAA. CDX verified: mcaa.gov.mn has archived PDFs (forms and regulatory docs from 2007-2010), but no accident investigation reports found in the archive. Mongolia has very low commercial accident volume. Major accidents typically investigated with ICAO Annex 13 assistance. No standalone public investigation report archive identified.

Uzbekistan Civil Aviation Authority (CAA) at caa.uz. CDX check for caa.uz was unreachable (CDX unverified). No known public online accident investigation report archive. Uzbekistan investigation body operates under the State Inspectorate on Supervision in Transport under MINTRANS. Reports are rarely published online; most findings disseminated through ICAO or bilateral channels. Extremely low expected record count for commercially registered aircraft accidents.

UAE GCAA (General Civil Aviation Authority) publishes accident/incident investigation reports at gcaa.gov.ae. English-language reports available. CDX confirms archived PDFs. Strong institutional capacity.
Israel AIAI (Accident Investigation and Inspection Authority) under Ministry of Transport publishes investigation reports at mot.gov.il/aviation. English reports available. Well-structured archive.
Turkey KAIK (Kaza Arastirma ve Inceleme Kurulu) under SHGM publishes reports at kaik.gov.tr. Mix of Turkish and English. Active investigation body with consistent publication cadence.
Saudi Arabia GACA/AIB (Accident Investigation Bureau) publishes reports at aib.gov.sa. Primarily Arabic; limited English translations. Archive is partial — older reports not always available online.
Egypt ECAA/CAOA (Civil Aviation Occurrence Authority) publishes at caoa.gov.eg. CDX confirms archived PDFs. Reports primarily in Arabic; coverage is partial. Active ICAO member with established AIB.
Qatar QCAA/AAIS (Accident and Incident Investigation Section) publishes at aais.com.qa. English-language reports available but archive is limited in depth. Small aviation volume.
Oman CAAR (Civil Aviation Authority Oman) publishes accident reports at caar.gov.om. Some English-language reports available. Archive is shallow; coverage improves post-2015.

Kuwait DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation) at dgca.gov.kw. CDX confirms archived PDFs including an accident/incident preliminary report form, but no investigation reports published publicly. Contact required for reports. Low aviation accident volume.

Bahrain CAA (Civil Aviation Affairs) at caa.gov.bh. No CDX-confirmed PDF archive. Limited public investigation report publication. Very low accident volume given small aviation market. Contact-based access only.

Jordan CARC (Civil Aviation Regulatory Commission) at carc.jo. CDX confirms archived PDFs (AIS/aerodrome docs); accident investigation reports also published. Arabic-primary with some English. Moderate institutional capacity.

Lebanon DGCA at dgca.gov.lb. No CDX-confirmed PDF archive. Institution severely disrupted by economic collapse and Beirut port explosion aftermath. Site intermittently accessible. Conflict/crisis classification; treat as unstable.

Iran CAO (Civil Aviation Organization) at cao.ir. CDX confirms archived PDFs with 200-status captures. Reports published in Farsi; access may be intermittent due to geo-blocking and sanctions. Wayback fallback viable given confirmed captures.

Iraq IQCAA severely capacity-constrained by conflict and institutional disruption. No CDX-confirmed public archive. mol.gov.iq CDX returns empty. Accident investigations rarely completed or published publicly.
Yemen YCAR effectively non-functional due to ongoing civil war since 2015. No online archive exists. Aviation activity severely curtailed; no realistic path to data acquisition.

Libya LCAA (Libyan Civil Aviation Authority) at lcaa.ly. CDX returns empty — no archived PDFs. Dual-government civil conflict since 2014 has paralysed the institution. No public investigation archive exists.
ATSB (Australian Transport Safety Bureau) publishes full investigation reports and occurrence database at atsb.gov.au; world-class open archive.
TAIC (Transport Accident Investigation Commission) publishes full reports at taic.org.nz; world-class open archive.
PNG AIC (Accident Investigation Commission) exists and publishes some reports; site stability inconsistent and CDX check timed out — wayback unverified.
Fiji CAAF (Civil Aviation Authority of Fiji) at caafi.org.fj; Wayback CDX confirmed PDF captures (2006–2007 org docs); investigation reports sparse.
Solomon Islands; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Vanuatu; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Samoa; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Tonga; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Kiribati; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Tuvalu; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Nauru; no national investigation capacity — accidents investigated by Australia ATSB as accredited representative.
Micronesia (FSM); US-affiliated (Compact of Free Association) — accidents investigated by US NTSB as accredited representative.
Marshall Islands; US-affiliated (Compact of Free Association) — accidents investigated by US NTSB as accredited representative.
Palau; US-affiliated (Compact of Free Association) — accidents investigated by US NTSB as accredited representative.
UK AAIB publishes full investigation reports and bulletins at aaib.gov.uk; CDX confirms archived PDFs.
France BEA publishes final reports at bea.aero; CDX confirms deep archived PDF catalogue.
Germany BFU publishes investigation reports at bfu-web.de; CDX confirms archived PDFs.
Italy ANSV publishes reports at ansv.it; CDX confirms archived PDFs; Italian-language content requires parsing.
Spain CIAIAC publishes reports at ciaiac.es; live archive confirmed; CDX found no archived PDFs (omitting wayback_target).
Netherlands DSB/OvV publishes reports at onderzoeksraad.nl; CDX confirms archived PDFs.
Belgium AAIU investigation reports accessible via mobilit.belgium.be; CDX confirms PDF captures.
Switzerland SUST publishes reports at sust.admin.ch; CDX confirms archived aviation investigation PDFs.
Austria SUB (Sicherheitsuntersuchungsstelle) reports under bmk.gv.at; CDX confirms PDFs on domain.
Portugal GPIAAF publishes reports at gpiaa.gov.pt; CDX confirms archived PDFs.
Ireland AAIU publishes reports at aaiu.ie; CDX confirms aviation investigation report PDFs archived.
Luxembourg AET publishes reports at aet.gouvernement.lu; CDX confirms aviation safety investigation PDF captures.
Greece AAIASB publishes reports at aaiasb.gr; CDX confirms PDF captures; smaller corpus, Greek-language content.
Sweden SHK (Statens haverikommission) publishes full investigation reports in English at shk.se going back to 1990s.
Norway NSIA (havarikommisjonen.no) publishes aviation investigation reports in English; successor to AIBN with full archive.
Finland OTKES (onnettomuustutkinta.fi) publishes aviation investigation reports in Finnish and English with public PDF archive.
Denmark AIB (hcl.dk / havarikommissionen.dk) publishes full investigation reports in Danish and English online.
Iceland RNSA (rnsa.is) publishes investigation PDFs; smaller archive, Icelandic-language reports. CDX verified: PDF captures 2019–2024.
Poland PKBWL (pkbwl.gov.pl) publishes aviation accident and serious incident reports publicly; English summaries available for major events.
Czech ÚZPLN (uzpln.cz) publishes aviation investigation reports online; Czech-language with some English summaries for major accidents.
Slovakia accident investigation handled by AAIB (nsat.sk / letectvo.mdv.sk); Slovak-language reports, smaller archive, moderate scraping effort.
Hungary KBSZ (kbsz.gov.hu) is the current authority; archive sparse online. Predecessor NKH had transport docs archived but no aviation-specific PDF index confirmed.
Romania CIAS (cias.gov.ro) publishes investigation reports; CDX shows PDF captures 2018–present but samples include press releases; full report archive accessible.
Bulgaria AIB (air-accidents.bg or within MTITC) publishes some reports online; smaller state, lower traffic volume, Bulgarian-language only.
Ukraine NBAAI (nbaai.gov.ua) had active archive pre-2022; site intermittently accessible during war. CDX verified: aviation safety analysis PDFs 2014–2019.
Georgia GCAA (gcaa.ge) has a web presence but CDX shows only AIP/regulatory PDFs, no investigation reports found publicly; contact-only for accident data.
Moldova CAA (caa.md) CDX shows only regulatory inspection checklists; no public accident investigation archive. Very low traffic volume state.
NTSB aviation accident database (ntsb.gov/aviation) covers 1962-present, structured CSV/API, world-class completeness.
TSB Canada (bst-tsb.gc.ca) publishes full investigation reports and searchable occurrence database, world-class.
SACAA (caa.co.za) Accident and Incident Investigation Division publishes PDF reports; structured data limited, HTML scraping needed.
DGAC Mexico (dgac.gob.mx) accident investigation section publishes PDF reports; coverage incomplete pre-2010, Spanish-only.
CCAA (ccaa.hr) publishes accident/incident investigation reports per EU Regulation 996/2010, EASA-aligned, English summaries available.
Serbian Directorate of Civil Aviation (cad.gov.rs) publishes investigation reports in Serbian; archive coverage partial, limited English content.
Slovenian CAA (caa.si) EU member, publishes investigation reports per 996/2010, EASA-aligned, relatively small fleet/occurrence count.
Estonian Safety Investigation Authority (juhtumid.ee / ecaa.ee) EU member, publishes structured investigation reports per 996/2010 with English versions.
Latvian CAA (caa.lv) EU member, publishes aviation accident investigation reports per EU Regulation 996/2010, English summaries available.
Lithuanian CAA (caa.lt) EU member, aviation safety investigation reports published per 996/2010; archive goes back to ~2004.
Cyprus DCA (mcw.gov.cy/dca) EU/EASA member, publishes investigation reports; small aviation sector limits record count.
Transport Malta (transport.gov.mt) EU/EASA member, MAAIB publishes investigation reports; very small island state fleet, low occurrence volume.
Albania CAA (caa.gov.al) Wayback has HR/admin PDFs only — no investigation reports confirmed; weak institution, contact-only for accident data.
N.Macedonia CAA (caa.gov.mk) Wayback has organogram/safety-program PDFs but no accident investigation reports; limited public archive.
Montenegro CAA (caa.me) Wayback has regulatory/instructor PDFs only — no investigation reports; small aviation sector, contact-only realistically.
Bosnia BHDCA (bhdca.gov.ba) Wayback confirmed safety report PDFs (2006 captures); live site unstable, archived reports exist for recovery via Wayback.
Bahamas AIP has limited own-published reports; major occurrences investigated by or co-reported with US NTSB given proximity and aviation ties.
Barbados DCA small Caribbean authority; significant accidents historically co-investigated with or cross-referenced to US NTSB/ECCAA-adjacent processes.
Haiti OFNAC has no functioning public accident archive; institutional capacity severely limited, occurrences may appear in ICAO ADREP or NTSB if US-registered aircraft.
Antigua & Barbuda ECCAA member; accident investigations typically handled via ECCAA regional framework or US NTSB for US-registered aircraft.
